message:
fix(web): improve haptic feedback with threshold-based triggering

Updates hapticLongPressBuildup to only fire haptics when crossing
threshold levels rather than on every progress update. This prevents
overwhelming haptic spam during long-press gestures and provides
cleaner tactile feedback at meaningful progress points.

Changes:
- Add lastThresholdRef parameter to track previously crossed threshold
- Define three threshold levels: 0.2 (selection), 0.4 (light), 0.7 (medium)
- Only trigger haptic when crossing to a higher threshold level
- Reset threshold tracking when progress decreases (user releases early)
- Return boolean indicating if haptic was triggered

Threshold behavior:
- 0-20%: No haptic (building anticipation)
- 20%: Selection haptic (subtle acknowledgment)
- 40%: Light haptic (progress confirmation)
- 70%: Medium haptic (approaching completion)

This matches premium iOS patterns where haptic feedback marks
meaningful state transitions rather than continuous progress.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
